audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle instruction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for freewill offering, sanctuary, ark, cover/mercy-seat, table, lampstand, and pattern shown on mountain.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, that they take for me a contribution/offering; from every person whose heart moves willingly, you shall take my contribution.
This is the contribution you shall take from them: gold, silver, bronze,
blue, purple, scarlet yarn, fine linen, goats' hair,
reddened ram skins, durable hides, acacia wood,
oil for light, spices for anointing oil and fragrant incense,
onyx stones and stones for setting, for ephod and breastpiece.
They shall make for me a sanctuary, and I will dwell in their midst.
According to all that I show you, pattern of dwelling-place and pattern of all its vessels, so you shall make it.
They shall make an ark of acacia wood: two and a half cubits its length, one and a half cubits its width, and one and a half cubits its height.
You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and outside you shall overlay it, and make for it a gold molding all around.
You shall cast four gold rings for it and set them on its four feet, two rings on one side and two rings on other side.
You shall make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
You shall bring poles into rings on sides of ark, to carry ark with them.
The poles shall remain in rings of ark; they shall not be removed from it.
You shall put into ark the testimony that I shall give you.
You shall make a cover/atonement-cover of pure gold: two and a half cubits its length and one and a half cubits its width.
You shall make two cherubim of gold; of hammered work you shall make them from two ends of cover.
Make one cherub from this end and one cherub from that end; from the cover you shall make the cherubim upon its two ends.
The cherubim shall be spreading wings upward, sheltering the cover with their wings, faces each toward the other; toward the cover shall faces of cherubim be.
You shall put the cover upon ark from above, and into ark you shall put testimony that I give you.
There I will meet with you, and I will speak with you from above the cover, from between two cherubim that are upon ark of testimony, all that I command you for children of Israel.
You shall make table of acacia wood: two cubits its length, one cubit its width, and one and a half cubits its height.
Overlay it with pure gold and make for it gold molding all around.
Make for it rim of handbreadth all around, and make gold molding for its rim all around.
Make for it four gold rings and put rings on four corners that are on its four feet.
Close to rim shall rings be, as housings for poles to carry table.
Make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold, and table shall be carried with them.
Make its dishes, pans, jars, and bowls with which to pour libations; of pure gold you shall make them.
You shall set bread of presence/faces on table before my face continually.
You shall make lampstand of pure gold; of hammered work lampstand shall be made, its base, stem, cups, bulbs, and blossoms from it.
Six branches shall go out from its sides, three branches of lampstand from one side and three from other side.
Three almond-blossom cups on one branch, bulb and blossom, and three almond-blossom cups on another branch, bulb and blossom; so for six branches going from lampstand.
On lampstand itself four almond-blossom cups, its bulbs and blossoms.
A bulb under two branches from it, a bulb under two branches from it, and a bulb under two branches from it, for six branches going from lampstand.
Their bulbs and branches shall be from it, all one hammered work of pure gold.
Make its seven lamps, and set up its lamps, so they give light before its face.
Its tongs and firepans shall be pure gold.
From a talent of pure gold he shall make it, with all these vessels.
See and make by their pattern, which you are shown on the mountain.

translation_notes: Tabernacle begins with willing-hearted contribution, not coercive extraction.
Mishkan/sanctuary is for Divine dwelling in the people's midst after liberation.
Ark poles remain: holy presence is portable with the wilderness people.
Cover/kapporet is meeting-speaking place; later 'mercy seat' theology should be audited but not overlaid too quickly.
Lampstand botanical almond imagery evokes life/tree symbolism.
safety_note: Tabernacle material should not be framed as wealth extraction. The opening condition is willing heart, and the purpose is presence in the midst of a liberated people.
